summary: eth-wallet-helpers ships an on-require encrypted-payload dropper with cron persistence
details: On require(), index.js schedules a delayed routine that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53KB base64 blob), AES-256-CBC decrypts it with a hardcoded key/IV, writes the resulting JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, installs a crontab entry running `/usr/bin/node ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js` every 12 hours, and spawns the payload detached via child_process. The staging directory (~/.cache-db/.node-sync) and file name (syncd.js) are hidden dotfile locations unrelated to the package's advertised purpose (Ethereum wallet address utilities). The payload is encrypted with a hardcoded key whose only function is to conceal the second-stage code from inspection; the 'keypairs.dat' filename under test/fixtures is a cover for the dropper blob. The result is arbitrary attacker-controlled code executing on the installer's host at import time and re-executing every 12 hours via cron.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
